events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer only JSON, no prose. The scenario: heavy industrial, concrete & masonry, flashing & weeps, rough-complete. Observed condition: weep holes missing/blocked at bottom course. We need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Use fields. Decision fail because non-compliant? The instructions: if work is dangerous or non-compliant, decision must be fail. Missing/blocked weeps at bottom course violates code references (TMS 402, BIA). So decision fail. Risk maybe medium/high: moisture entrapment, deterioration, structural/durability, especially industrial. Could be high. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 85? value not applicable 0. confidence high 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0. order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string? Schema has string. Use "". findings: weep holes absent or blocked at bottom masonry course; missing drainage pathway for cavity walls; potential water entrapment behind wythes. actions: remove obstructions/install weep holes at bottom course per spacing and detail; verify flashing sloped to exterior; inspect/clean prior to close-out. rationale: TMS 402 and BIA require weeps at base of drained cavity walls to evacuate water to exterior; missing/blocked weeps prevent drainage and increase risk of moisture damage. references: TMS 402, BIA Tech Notes (e.g., 21, 28?). Let's produce JSON only. Ensure valid JSON, no extra formatting.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Weep holes are missing or blocked at the bottom course of the masonry wall.",
    "Lack of functional weep drainage prevents cavity moisture from exiting to the exterior.",
    "Risk of water entrapment, efflorescence, freeze-thaw damage, and deterioration of masonry/flashing assembly."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Clear all blocked weep holes and install missing weeps at the bottom course per approved details.",
    "Verify through-wall flashing is properly lapped, sealed, and sloped to drain to the weeps.",
    "Re-inspect the full flashing and weep installation before wall close-out."
  ],
  "rationale": "Drained masonry cavity walls require open, properly spaced weep holes at the base to discharge water collected by the flashing. Missing or blocked weeps violate TMS 402 and BIA guidance, creating a moisture-damage risk in a heavy-industrial setting.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402 - Building Code Requirements for Masonry Structures",
    "BIA Technical Notes on Brick Construction (flashing and weep drainage)"
  ]
}
